flows.name: Fresh data-only install
flows.summary: A maintainer installs Architext into a repository without copying viewer implementation files.
flows.trigger: Maintainer runs architext sync [path] in a repository with no Architext data.

steps.summary: CLI resolves the optional path argument or defaults to the current directory.
CLI writes neutral starter data under docs/architext/data.
CLI writes docs/architext/.architext.json with install state.
Package-owned schemas validate the starter model.

guarantees: Target repo does not receive copied viewer, schema, tool, package, or Vite files.
failureBehavior: Invalid starter data fails sync before claiming installation health.
knownGaps: Install prompt UX still needs real user testing on Windows shells.

flows.name: Copied install migration
flows.summary: An existing copied-template install is converted into the 1.0 data-only layout.
flows.trigger: Maintainer runs architext sync [path] where docs/architext contains copied implementation files.

steps.summary: CLI detects package-owned files such as src, schema, tools, package.json, and Vite config.
CLI leaves docs/architext/data/*.json untouched unless overwrite is explicit.
CLI replaces old Architext agent sections with global-CLI guidance while preserving unrelated instructions.
CLI removes copied viewer, schema, tool, package, and Vite files from the target repository.
CLI validates preserved target data against package-owned schemas.

guarantees: Architecture facts are not rewritten silently.
Agent instructions no longer tell agents to edit copied package-owned files.
failureBehavior: Dry-run exposes destructive removals; validation failure points to repair prompt.
knownGaps: Instruction replacement is intentionally marker-based and does not parse arbitrary Markdown ASTs.

flows.name: Architecture data maintenance
flows.summary: An LLM agent updates architecture data in response to real repository changes.
flows.trigger: A code change alters architecture, persistence, data flow, external integrations, deployment, risks, or decisions.

steps.summary: Agent reads the managed Architext section and any project-specific constraints.
Agent reads existing JSON before editing.
Agent updates only project-owned data files and preserves stable IDs.
Agent runs package-owned validation before claiming completion.

guarantees: Package-owned viewer/schema/tool files are not edited in target repositories.
failureBehavior: If validation fails, agent repairs JSON or reports the failure loudly.
knownGaps: Generated architecture claims still require human review.

flows.name: Local viewer review
flows.summary: A maintainer serves the package-owned viewer against target repository data.

steps.summary: CLI starts the package-owned Vite server with target data directory configured.
Viewer assets are served from the installed package.
Viewer fetches /data/*.json from the selected target repository.

guarantees: Review requires a local server but not target-local npm dependencies.
failureBehavior: Missing data produces viewer load errors and doctor guidance.
knownGaps: The viewer remains a local dev server in normal review mode.

flows.name: Static export
flows.summary: A maintainer builds a static viewer that includes a copy of selected target data.

steps.summary: CLI builds package-owned viewer assets into target docs/architext/dist.
CLI copies target data into dist/data for static serving.

guarantees: Generated static output can be served without Vite.
failureBehavior: Stale dist output is treated as generated and can be cleaned.
knownGaps: Static export can become stale after data edits.

flows.name: PDF export
flows.summary: A maintainer exports selected Architext views as a portable PDF for review, archival, or offline sharing.
flows.trigger: Maintainer requests a PDF export from the CLI or viewer.

steps.summary: Maintainer chooses whether to export the active view, all views, or a report bundle.
CLI renders package-owned viewer output or a print-specific document into a PDF artifact.

guarantees: PDF output should not require copied viewer files in the target repository.
failureBehavior: Missing fonts, oversized diagrams, or route warnings should fail loudly or appear in export diagnostics.
knownGaps: Export approach is undecided: headless browser print, print-specific renderer, or both.

flows.name: Release packaging
flows.summary: Architext ships the global CLI, package-owned viewer, schemas, and starter templates as a major semver release.

steps.summary: Maintainer runs validation and production build.
Maintainer verifies npm package contents exclude target generated artifacts.

guarantees: 1.0.0 marks the breaking distribution model change.
knownGaps: A CI workflow is still needed to enforce packaging checks automatically.
